items.headline: US Futures Dip After Thursday's Record Highs Amid Inflation Concerns
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are showing declines on Friday, May 15, following Thursday's record-setting performance across major indices. Investor sentiment is being weighed down by persistent inflation fears, escalating oil prices, and geopolitical uncertainties, despite continued strong performance from AI-driven technology stocks. The anticipated transition of the Federal Reserve chairmanship is also a key area of market focus today. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Closed up 0.77% Thursday, futures down 1.09% Friday
Closed up 0.88% Thursday, Nasdaq 100 futures down 1.54% Friday
Closed up 0.75% Thursday, futures down 0.67% Friday

items.headline: Figma Surges on Strong Q1 Results and AI Adoption
items.summary: Shares of Figma (FIG) jumped over 10% in premarket trading after the company reported stronger-than-expected first-quarter results and raised its full-year sales guidance, largely attributed to broad seat expansion and growing adoption of its AI products.

items.headline: Cisco Systems Rallies on Robust Earnings and Restructuring
items.summary: Cisco Systems (CSCO) saw a significant increase on Thursday following the release of stronger-than-expected quarterly results and an announcement of job cuts.

items.headline: NVIDIA Continues AI-Driven Ascent, Gains on Chip Approvals
items.summary: NVIDIA (NVDA) extended its gains on Thursday, propelled by sustained investor enthusiasm for AI infrastructure and chip demand, further boosted by reports of U.S. clearance for H200 chip shipments to Chinese firms.

items.headline: Papa John's Rises on Potential Takeover Bid
items.summary: Papa John's (PZZA) shares rose Friday morning after reports surfaced that its largest franchisee plans to join a potential takeover bid for the pizza chain.

items.headline: Applied Materials Dips Despite Strong Earnings, Trade Concerns Linger
items.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) was down in premarket trading despite reporting better-than-expected second-quarter financial results and issuing strong third-quarter guidance, potentially reflecting broader concerns over semiconductor trade restrictions.

items.headline: Boeing Shares Decline Amid Broader Market Weakness
items.summary: Boeing Co. (BA) experienced a decline in Thursday's trading session.

items.headline: Amazon Shares Edge Lower
items.summary: Amazon (AMZN) saw its shares decline on Thursday.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Leadership Transition Underway: Warsh to Replace Powell
items.summary: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair concludes today, May 15, with Kevin Warsh anticipated to assume the role. This leadership change is being closely watched by markets, as Warsh's historical stance suggests a potential shift in the Fed's approach to inflation policy, which could introduce market volatility. The CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a 99.4% probability of the Federal Reserve maintaining current interest rates in June.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductors Lead as Consumer Sectors Face Headwinds
items.summary: Technology and semiconductor stocks continue to drive market performance, fueled by robust demand and investment in Artificial Intelligence. Conversely, sectors like Consumer Discretionary and Consumer Staples are experiencing pressure from elevated inflation, rising energy costs, and higher interest rates, impacting consumer spending habits.

items.headline: Inflation Remains Elevated; Retail Sales Show Strength
items.summary: Recent data shows wholesale prices surged 6% annually, and core CPI remained well above the Federal Reserve's 2% target in April. Despite persistent inflation, U.S. retail sales recorded a third consecutive monthly increase in April, signaling resilient consumer spending. The 10-year Treasury bond is yielding 4.54%, with the two-year bond at 4.06%. The Empire State Manufacturing survey for May and April's industrial production and capacity utilization reports are due later today.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Oil Prices Influence Market Sentiment
items.summary: Global markets continue to be sensitive to geopolitical developments, including the ongoing Iran conflict, which has contributed to rising oil prices with Brent crude above $107 per barrel and WTI crude near $105 per barrel. Additionally, the outcomes of US-China trade discussions are influencing market sentiment and impacting sectors like semiconductors.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings, More Expected Today
items.summary: Figma ($FIG), Applied Materials ($AMAT), Winnebago Industries ($WGO), and Cisco Systems ($CSCO) recently reported stronger-than-expected earnings. Companies scheduled to release their earnings reports today, May 15, include Diana Shipping ($DSX), RBC Bearings ($RBC), Sachem Capital ($SACH), Skillz ($SKLZ), ARS Pharmaceuticals ($SPRY), SurgePays ($SURG), and VolitionRx Limited.
